title: Persistent eastbound surface current
advice: A surface current flows continuously E at 2-3 kt regardless of tide (Atlantic-Mediterranean density difference). Aids eastbound at all times; manage carefully westbound. North (Spanish) shore ~+0.5 kt, south (Moroccan) shore ~+1 kt.

title: Eastbound: depart Barbate ~3h before HW Gibraltar
advice: E-going tidal stream begins ~HW Gibraltar (= HW Tarifa) mid-Strait, earlier near shores. Depart Barbate ~3h before HW Gibraltar to reach Tarifa at slack/start of E-going stream for the ~6h favourable window. Stay mid-channel. Avoid Levante (E wind) — wait for Poniente or calm.
seasonNote: Levante can blow for days; persistent cloud over the Rock signals it.

title: Westbound: depart Gibraltar ~2h after HW Tarifa
advice: W-going stream begins ~6h after HW Tarifa mid-Strait. Depart La Línea/Gibraltar ~2h after HW Tarifa to catch it; pass near the European (N) shore for stronger inshore W-going stream. Window is shorter than eastbound (~2-4h) and must overcome the persistent E-going surface current.
seasonNote: Levante assists westbound but builds dangerous steep seas against the stream.

title: Douro bar — swell + ebb hazard; enter near HW
advice: Enter on the last 2h of rising tide. Bar ~6 m at HW, dangerous at LW. River ebb ~3 kt at springs; with onshore swell creates breaking waves. Avoid entry/exit with S wind + swell >2.5 m. Follow buoyed channel; sandbanks to starboard. Call marina before entry.
seasonNote: Worst in winter / high river flow.

title: Aveiro bar — enter near HW; commercial traffic
advice: Northern breakwater extension improved conditions, but enter near HW with swell. Active commercial port. Inside: unlit HT cables (8 m clearance) across Canal da Mira. Call port authority before entering.

title: Figueira bar — standing waves on ebb with swell
advice: Enter the last 2h of rising tide. Spring ebb up to 6 kt (winter); onshore swell + ebb = dangerous standing waves. Port closure signals from Forte de Santa Catarina (R-G-R lights / black ball = closed). Entrance dredged min 7 m. Figueira Pilots VHF 14 (16 in poor vis).
seasonNote: Most dangerous in winter onshore gales; summer with W swell <1.5 m usually manageable.

title: Ria Formosa inlet — 4 kt current; flood entry only
advice: Depth-averaged current >2 m/s (~4 kt) at springs. Enter on the flood; aim to arrive ~2h before HW Olhão. HW at the inner quay lags the entrance ~30-45 min. Buoyed channel (min 3 m at LW) but marks shift — navigate from chart. Use the main Faro-Olhão inlet.

title: Guadiana bar — shallow, shifting; second half of flood
advice: Bar shallow and variable (draft >2 m may ground at LW springs); sandbanks shift after rain/weather. Enter on the second half of the rising tide near slack to avoid cross-currents. At HW, depths typically >3 m.

title: Guadalquivir bar — NEVER enter with W wind + ebb
advice: Never transit with W wind and falling tide; bar shallow (~2.6 m reported) and swell + ebb = breaking waves. Enter near HW on the turn of tide via the buoyed funnel channel. ~8h motor up to the Bonanza lock (Seville) on the flood.
seasonNote: SW swell most dangerous in autumn/winter.

title: Cabo Trafalgar — tidal race + shoals
advice: Severe tidal race extends SW from the cape on strong E-going flow; the Los Cabezos shoal plateau west of Tarifa makes a race of considerable violence. Keep 3-5 M offshore in adverse tidal conditions.
seasonNote: Worst with E-going spring stream against contrary swell.

title: Cabo de São Vicente — wind acceleration + headland race
advice: SW corner of mainland Europe. Strong wind acceleration around the headland, especially in the summer NW Nortada (15-25 kt accelerating to 30+). Tidal stream and swell from all W quadrants make short steep seas in wind-against-stream. Keep 3+ M offing; round at dawn before the wind builds.
seasonNote: Summer Nortada reliable and strong; pre-dawn rounding recommended.

title: Cabo Finisterre — lee shore + orca zone
advice: The Paso de Fisterra is exposed to the full NW fetch of the North Atlantic; overfalls in wind-against-tide. Also a documented orca-interaction hotspot (GTOA) — keep 6+ kt and hug the 20 m contour inshore where advised; check the CA/GTOA map before passage.
seasonNote: Orca interactions peak late summer / autumn.

title: Almadraba tuna traps — Apr-Jun navigation hazard
advice: Labyrinth net systems extend >=1 km offshore at Conil (~36 16N 6 06W), Barbate (~36 10N 5 55W), Zahara (~36 08N 5 51W) and Tarifa (~36 01N 5 37W). Set after the first full moon in May, fished through June; marked by surface floats. Pass 1-2 M clear to seaward. Barbate nets sit close to the eastbound Strait approach.
